title_of_original: Judgment of the Court of Justice of 24 January 1978, Van Tiggele, 82/77
decided_by: Court of Justice of the European Union
case_description: [1] On 12 May 2018, the Italian Organisation for Digital Entertainment Markets (AMID), [2] a public regulatory body established under Italian law to oversee the distribution of digital media, [3] adopted a Retail Pricing Regulation for Video Games. [4] Approved by the Ministry for Economic Development on 15 May 2018, the regulation came into force in June 2018. [5] It was adopted pursuant to Ministerial Decree No. 28/2018, [6] which authorised AMID to introduce measures aimed at stabilising the market for consumer video game distribution for a period of four years. [7] Under the regulation, minimum retail prices were set for various categories of digital video games. [8] For example, the price of standard console games was calculated based on the publisher's list price plus a fixed mark-up of €3.00 and applicable VAT, with an absolute minimum of €34.99 per title. [9] If no publisher list price was available (e.g. for independently developed or imported titles), [10] the minimum price automatically defaulted to €34.99. [11] These pricing rules applied equally to games developed in Italy and imported games sold on the Italian market. [11] The official rationale for the regulation was to curb aggressive undercutting following the collapse of the informal pricing agreements [12] that had previously been used by large Italian game publishers and retailers. [13] According to the Italian authorities, the minimum pricing system was necessary to prevent a 'race to the bottom' in retail pricing, [14] which could threaten the viability of small and medium-sized digital retailers and weaken the domestic market structure. [15] As intended, the regulation eliminated price competition at the consumer price level [16] and ensured a minimum margin for retailers. [17] However, it also increased the cost of games for consumers, [18] particularly for titles that could otherwise have been offered at lower prices by independent developers or via international online platforms.
gold_parsing: was_designated('AMID', 'minimum retail price', 'ministry for economic development'). % [4],[5],[6]
